ingredients.name: Imamiya Shrine
ingredients.address: 21 Murasakino Imamiyacho, Kita Ward, Kyoto, 603-8243, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Imamiya Shrine in Kyoto's Kita ward appears in Episode 2 as part of a character's memories from a previous relationship, its weathered stone torii and the smell of aburi-mochi standing for a happier past that the protagonist is trying to move beyond.

ingredients.name: Atami Station (Bus)
ingredients.address: ,[null,null,35.103842199999995,139.0778841],null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,
ingredients.entityContext: Atami Station serves as the gateway moment in Episode 11 when one of the leads almost boards a bus to leave Atami for good, only to be stopped at the last moment in a scene fans call the show's emotional turning point.

ingredients.name: nagisArt café
ingredients.address: nagisArt café, ACAO FOREST 2階 1027-8 Kamitaga, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0101, Japan

ingredients.name: Shima cafe MAKANA
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒238-0237 Kanagawa, Miura, Misakimachi, Jogashima−105 Shima cafe MAKANA
ingredients.entityContext: This island cafe on Jogashima in Episode 8 is where two of the characters eat lunch during a day excursion, the sea view and laid-back vibe prompting an unexpectedly personal exchange about what they each want from life after Atami.

ingredients.name: Sushikan Atami
ingredients.localName: うまい鮨勘 熱海支店
ingredients.address: Sushikan Atami, 1-2 Chuocho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 6 the two leads share a counter seat at Sushikan and watch the chef work while attempting a serious conversation that keeps getting derailed by incredible food, a scene that fans remember for its mix of intimacy and comedy.

ingredients.name: Hotel Micuras
ingredients.address: Hotel Micuras, 3 Higashikaiganchō, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hotel Micuras, a sleek seaside hotel right on Atami's main beach, provides the setting for Episode 5's pivotal hotel-lobby scene where the protagonists first admit, in oblique terms, that their time together in Atami means more than either planned.

ingredients.name: FUKUROU Atami
ingredients.localName: 創作和食 ふくろう 熱海店
ingredients.address: FUKUROU Atami, 7-34 Sakimicho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0019, Japan

ingredients.name: ATAMI Funny house
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0003 Shizuoka, Atami, Kaikocho, 4−10 ATAMI Funny house

ingredients.name: Atami Izusan Hotel Suiyotei
ingredients.address: Atami Izusan Hotel Suiyōtei, 190-1 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional ryokan in Izusan is where the group stays overnight in Episode 11 for a farewell trip, the tatami rooms and ocean views giving the evening a quietly ceremonial feeling as the characters accept that their Atami chapter is ending.

ingredients.name: Izusan Post Office
ingredients.address: Izusan Post Office, 579 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small Izusan Post Office features in Episode 11 as part of the characters' final morning routine in the Izusan area, a mundane local stop that carries unexpected emotional weight because it signals how fully they have settled into life in this corner of Atami.

ingredients.name: Atamishi Nakamichi Community Center
ingredients.localName: 熱海市 仲道公民館
ingredients.address: Atamishi Nakamichi Community Center, 707 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Nakamichi community center in Izusan appears in Episode 11 in the background of a neighbourhood walk scene, grounding the story in the real residential texture of Atami beyond the tourist-facing waterfront.

ingredients.name: Izusan Shrine
ingredients.address: Izusan Shrine, 708-1 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Perched above Atami with stone steps threading through old cedar trees, Izusan Shrine is where Episode 11's climactic confession scene takes place, the characters finally saying aloud what the entire series has been building toward. Pilgrims recreate the stairway shot as the definitive emotional landmark of the show.

ingredients.name: 熱海第一交通(株)配車センター
ingredients.localName: 熱海第一交通(株)配車センター
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0015 Shizuoka, Atami, Chuocho, 14−26 熱海第一交通(株)配車センター

ingredients.name: Izusan Port
ingredients.address: Izusan Port, 216 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small Izusan fishing port appears in Episode 10 as a quiet spot where one of the leads comes alone to think, watching boats bob in the water in a solitary scene that signals how close the story is to its resolution.

ingredients.name: Hatsushima Fisheries Cooperative Supermarket
ingredients.address: A-Coop, 2377-1 Miyanoura, Yakushima, Kumage District, Kagoshima 891-4205, Japan

ingredients.name: Shima No Yu Hot Spring
ingredients.address: Shima No Yu Hot Spring, 18 Hatsushima, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The public hot-spring bath on Hatsushima island is where the group soaks after their adventure-course session in Episode 8, a relaxed scene that cements the day-trip as the emotional midpoint of the series.

ingredients.name: Atami Ekimae Shinko Hall
ingredients.address: Atami Ekimae Shinko Hall, 8-16 Tawarahoncho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This community hall near the station hosts a local event in Episode 8 that the group attends almost by accident, a small-town scene that highlights how deeply Atami feels like home to the characters who have been there long enough to stumble into its civic life.

ingredients.name: Nakamise-dori Shopping Street
ingredients.address: Nakamise Shopping Street, 1 Chome-36-3 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous Asakusa tourist arcade appears in Episode 8 as a recalled Tokyo landmark, one character mentioning a past trip there as they contrast the loud tourist energy of Nakamise with the slower pace they have come to love in Atami.

ingredients.name: Hatsushima Lighthouse
ingredients.address: Hatsushima Lighthouse, 444 Hatsushima, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The lighthouse at the tip of Hatsushima island appears in Episode 8 during the group's day trip, a picturesque endpoint to their walk around the island that prompts an unexpectedly candid exchange between the two leads about what they each left behind on the mainland.

ingredients.name: Atami Geigi Kenban
ingredients.address: Atami Geigi Kenban, 17-13 Chuocho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Atami's geisha training hall appears in Episode 8 when the group catches a glimpse of a geiko performance during a local festival, a scene that underscores Atami's identity as a resort town with deep traditional entertainment roots.

ingredients.name: Sugimoto Pharmacy
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒247-0056 Kanagawa, Kamakura, Ofuna, 1 Chome−25−37 漢方杉本薬局
ingredients.entityContext: This Kamakura herbal medicine shop appears in Episode 7 tied to a side character's errand, a small grounding detail that places the story briefly outside Atami and signals that lives and obligations continue beyond the resort.

ingredients.name: Atami Ekimae Post Office
ingredients.address: Atami Ekimae Post Office, 4-10 Tawarahoncho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The post office just outside Atami Station appears in Episode 7 when a character mails a letter home, a quiet scene that highlights the theme of communication and all the things the protagonists struggle to say to the people who care about them.

ingredients.name: Itokawa Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0015 Shizuoka, Atami, Ginzacho, 4 Itokawa Bridge, 銀座
ingredients.entityContext: Itokawa Bridge spans the Itokawa River just a short walk from Ginza Street, and in Episode 7 the protagonists pause here at night under the streetlights, the bridge's reflection in the water accompanying a conversation about whether their time together has an expiry date.

ingredients.name: Hatsushima Adventure "SARUTOBI"
ingredients.localName: 初島アドベンチャー SARUTOBI
ingredients.address: Hatsushima Adventure "SARUTOBI", 1113 Hatsushima, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The treetop rope-course on Hatsushima provides Episode 8's lightest moment, with the characters helping each other across the suspended walkways in a scene that lets them be physically close in a context that is socially unambiguous, much to their mutual relief.

ingredients.name: Hatsushima Harbour
ingredients.localName: 初島港(シマテラス初島)
ingredients.address: Hatsushima Harbour, 209 Hatsushima, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The compact port on Hatsushima island is where the group arrives by ferry in Episode 8 for a spontaneous island day trip, a scene that briefly strips away the Atami setting and lets the characters act looser and more honest with each other.

ingredients.name: Ujigami Jinja
ingredients.address: Yamada-５９ Uji, Kyoto 611-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ujigami Jinja in Uji, the oldest surviving Shinto shrine building in Japan, appears in Episode 8 as one of the locations recalled by a character who spent time in Kyoto before coming to Atami, grounding her backstory in a place of remarkable antiquity.

ingredients.name: Tenjino Bridge
ingredients.address: Kintaikyo Bridge, 2 Iwakuni, Yamaguchi 741-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous Kintaikyo arched bridge in Iwakuni appears in Episode 4 imagery linked to one character's past travels, its five graceful wooden spans serving as a recurring visual motif for journeys taken before the characters found each other in Atami.

ingredients.name: Tokai Dried Fish
ingredients.entityContext: Tokai Dried Fish, a traditional himono shop in central Atami, appears in the opening credits montage as a street-level detail anchoring the show's portrait of everyday Atami commerce alongside its resort glamour.

ingredients.name: Atami Sun Beach
ingredients.address: Atami Sun Beach, 地先 Higashikaigancho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The sun-washed crescent of Atami Sun Beach anchors the opening credits sequence, its palm-lined promenade the visual shorthand for the bittersweet fresh start both protagonists are chasing. Fans arrive here first to feel the exact frame that opens the show.

ingredients.name: Densuke Chaya
ingredients.localName: 海のお食事処 でん助茶屋
ingredients.address: Densuke Chaya, 14-66 Higashikaigancho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This seafood chaya right on the beachfront is where the two characters eat their first shared meal in Episode 1, ordering whatever is fresh and splitting it without ceremony, a low-key scene that establishes the easy rhythm they will eventually build on.

ingredients.name: Ieyasu-no-yu
ingredients.address: Ieyasu-no-yu, 11-1 Tawarahoncho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The free footbath near the Atami Sun Beach boardwalk is where the two leads soak their feet together in Episode 1 at the end of their first day of tentative companionship, a small shared indulgence that sets the warm, low-stakes tone of their early dynamic.

ingredients.name: Atami Heiwa-dōri Shopping Street
ingredients.localName: 熱海駅前平和通り名店街
ingredients.address: Atami Heiwa-dōri Shopping Street, 4-1 Tawarahoncho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Heiwa-dori covered arcade near the station is where the protagonists browse Atami omiyage shops in Episode 1 on their first walk together, the fluorescent-lit, cheerfully retro street contrasting with their still-guarded body language.

ingredients.name: Itoen Hotel Atami Building
ingredients.localName: 伊東園ホテル 熱海館
ingredients.address: Itoen Hotel Atami Building, 4-16 Tawarahoncho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0011, Japan

ingredients.name: Atami Shave Ice MIYASAKA
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0018 Shizuoka, Atami, Kamijukucho, 13−8 熱海シェーブアイスMIYASAKA

ingredients.name: Atami Ginza Shopping Street
ingredients.address: Atami Ginza Shopping Street, 5-11 Ginzacho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The two leads stroll through this covered shopping arcade in Episode 1, buying snacks and trading awkward small talk that marks the very start of their reluctant friendship. The retro shotengai storefronts give the scene its warm, unhurried Atami atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Shin Kado Ya
ingredients.localName: 熱海の癒 新かどや
ingredients.address: Shin Kado Ya, 14-8 Koarashicho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0029, Japan

ingredients.name: Atami Water Park
ingredients.address: Atami Water Park, 10 Nagisacho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This seaside park along the waterfront is where the protagonists sit on the stone steps and watch the ocean in Episode 1, sharing the first quiet moment that cracks open both of their guarded moods. The view toward the bay makes it one of the most photographed pilgrimage stops.

ingredients.name: Times Car Rental Atami City-Operated Second Water Park
ingredients.localName: タイムズカー タイムズ熱海市営第２親水公園
ingredients.address: Times Car Rental, 14-14 Nagisacho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0014, Japan

ingredients.name: Shigemura Guesthouse Inn
ingredients.address: guesthouse SHIBAFU, 1 Chome-15-22 Hase, Kamakura, Kanagawa 248-0016, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kamakura guesthouse where the protagonist stayed before Atami is glimpsed in an Episode 1 flashback, a deliberately ordinary backdrop that makes the move to the seaside resort feel like a deliberate break rather than just a change of address.

ingredients.name: New Fujiya Hotel Annex
ingredients.localName: 熱海ニューフジヤホテル アネックス
ingredients.address: New Fujiya Hotel Annex, 3-25 Kamijukucho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0018, Japan

ingredients.name: Seafood Rice Bowl × Creative Japanese Cuisine Ren
Namiryo Bridge
ingredients.address: Nusamai Bridge, 1 Kitaodori, Kushiro, Hokkaido 085-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive Nusamai Bridge in Kushiro appears in an Episode 2 flashback that reveals one protagonist's life before Atami, the wide Hokkaido river and the bridge's ornate green ironwork marking a visual world utterly different from the warm coastal resort.

ingredients.name: 宮坂 Miyasaka
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒107-0062 Tokyo, Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 5 Chome−4−30 E号 宮坂 Miyasaka

ingredients.name: Cafe&Restaurant Nagisa
ingredients.address: Cafe&Restaurant Nagisa, 10-5 Nagisacho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0014, Japan

ingredients.name: Shabuyo Atami
ingredients.address: Shabuyo Atami, 18-10 Kamijukucho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0018, Japan

ingredients.name: Kiunkaku
ingredients.address: Kiunkaku, 4-2 Showacho, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic Kiunkaku villa becomes the backdrop for a pivotal Episode 3 date where the characters wander through tatami rooms and Meiji-era gardens, each trying to interpret whether the outing is more than just sightseeing. The layered architecture mirrors the slow peeling-back of their emotional defenses.

ingredients.name: Hashiri-yu
ingredients.address: Hashiri-yu, 604-10 Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hashiri-yu is one of Japan's oldest hot springs, gushing horizontally from a sea-cliff cave, and the characters visit it together in Episode 3 as an oddly intimate detour that deepens their connection through shared wonder at something ancient and strange.

ingredients.name: Izu-Taga Station
ingredients.address: Izu-Taga Station, 75 Kamitaga, 熱海市 静岡県 413-0101, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Izu-Taga Station on the scenic Izukyu line appears in Episode 4 as the stop nearest to ACAO FOREST, with the small platform and surrounding greenery marking a rare moment when the characters travel together away from central Atami.

ingredients.name: Kinomiya Station
ingredients.address: Kinomiya Station, 7 Fukumichichō, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0017, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kinomiya Station, the small Izukyu line stop closest to Kinomiya Shrine, is used in Episode 4 as a transit point when a character makes an unplanned side trip, the unhurried rural platform reinforcing how far they have drifted from their old urban routines.

ingredients.name: Esperanza Resort Atami
ingredients.localName: エスペランサリゾート熱海
ingredients.address: Esperanza Resort Atami, Kaikoucho, 1, Atami, Shizuoka 413-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This boutique hotel near the Atami waterfront appears in Episode 4 as a location one character visits to meet someone from their past, a scene that temporarily disrupts the comfortable rhythm they have built with the protagonist.

ingredients.name: atami onsen guesthouse nagomi
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0016 Shizuoka, Atami, Minaguchicho, 2 Chome−18−11 atami onsen guesthouse nagomi

ingredients.name: Kamijuku Bridge
ingredients.address: Shinkyo (Sacred Bridge), 1 Yoyogikamizonocho, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0052, Japan

ingredients.name: Hanano Yosei
ingredients.address: Hanano Yosei, 1993-65 Atami, Shizuoka 413-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This flower-themed cafe in Atami's hills appears in Episode 5 as a spot one character discovers on a solo walk and returns to with the other lead as a kind of quiet peace offering, the garden terrace giving the reconciliation scene a gentle, unforced beauty.

ingredients.name: 352-2 Nagisacho Parking
ingredients.localName: アットパーク熱海サンビーチ

ingredients.name: 熱海コンパニオン ジャケット
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒413-0015 Shizuoka, Atami, Chuocho, 7−12 熱海コンパニオン ジャケット
